tools.description: Convert a text, code, HTML, or document file to Markdown format. Supports HTML/HTM/MHTML/webarchive; documents PDF, DOCX, DOC, RTF, ODT; spreadsheets XLSX, XLS, ODS, CSV; presentations PPTX, ODP; email EML; ebooks EPUB, MOBI; RSS/Atom feeds; and markup WIKI, RST, ADOC, ORG, TEX, TEXTILE.

add_line_numbers.description: Add line numbers to code block (default: false)

analyze_headings.description: Analyze heading structure and hierarchy (default: false)

convert_tables.description: Convert HTML tables to Markdown pipe tables (default: false)

extract_blockquotes.description: Extract and convert HTML blockquotes (default: false)

extract_definition_lists.description: Extract and convert HTML definition lists (default: false)

extract_forms.description: Extract HTML forms and convert to Markdown tables (default: false)

extract_images.description: Extract and process images from HTML (default: false)

extract_links.description: Extract and summarize all links in document (default: false)

extract_metadata.description: Extract metadata from HTML files as YAML frontmatter (default: false)

file_path.description: Path to the file to convert

generate_toc.description: Generate table of contents from headings (default: false)

image_format.description: Image output format: 'link' (external URLs), 'skip' (remove), or 'embed' (base64, default: 'link')

include_filename.description: Include filename as Markdown heading (default: true)

max_bytes.description: Size limit in bytes for structured formats (default 10485760 = 10MB); larger plain-text files stream instead

preserve_comments.description: Preserve HTML comments in output (default: false)

preserve_css_hints.description: Preserve CSS styling hints as HTML comments in output (default: false)

toc_max_level.description: Maximum heading level to include in TOC (1-6, default: 3)

tools.name: convert_file

tools.description: Convert plain text content to Markdown format

add_line_numbers.description: Add line numbers to code block (default: false)

content.description: The text content to convert

title.description: Optional: title for the markdown document

tools.name: convert_text

tools.description: Convert code or HTML from various sources (file, URL, stdin) to Markdown

add_line_numbers.description: Add line numbers to code block (default: false)

analyze_headings.description: Analyze heading structure and hierarchy (default: false)

convert_tables.description: Convert HTML tables to Markdown pipe tables (default: false)

extract_blockquotes.description: Extract and convert HTML blockquotes (default: false)

extract_definition_lists.description: Extract and convert HTML definition lists (default: false)

extract_forms.description: Extract HTML forms and convert to Markdown tables (default: false)

extract_images.description: Extract and process images from HTML (default: false)

extract_links.description: Extract and summarize all links in document (default: false)

extract_metadata.description: Extract metadata from HTML as YAML frontmatter (default: false)

file_type.description: Optional: specify code language (auto-detected from file extension if omitted)

generate_toc.description: Generate table of contents from headings (default: false)

image_format.description: Image output format: 'link' (external URLs), 'skip' (remove), or 'embed' (base64, default: 'link')

preserve_comments.description: Preserve HTML comments in output (default: false)

preserve_css_hints.description: Preserve CSS styling hints as HTML comments (default: false)

source.description: Source: file path, URL (http://...), or '-' for stdin

title.description: Optional: title for the markdown document

toc_max_level.description: Maximum heading level to include in TOC (1-6, default: 3)

tools.name: convert_from_source

tools.description: List all code files in a directory (recursively)

directory.description: Directory path to scan for code files

tools.name: list_directory_files

tools.description: Get a lightweight summary of a file including metadata, headings, and preview

file_path.description: Path to the file to summarize

preview_length.description: Number of characters to include in preview (default: 300)

tools.name: get_file_summary

tools.description: Convert multiple files to Markdown in one call

convert_tables.description: Convert HTML tables to Markdown (default: false)

extract_metadata.description: Extract metadata from HTML files (default: false)

file_paths.description: List of file paths to convert (up to 10)

tools.name: batch_convert_files

tools.description: Search for text across files and return matching snippets

max_results.description: Maximum number of files with matches (default: 5)

tools.name: search_files

tools.description: List recently modified files in a directory

limit.description: Maximum number of files to return (default: 10)

tools.name: get_recently_modified_files

tools.description: Returns statistics about a directory: file counts by type, top-level folder list, and top 20 most frequent tags from Markdown files
tools.name: get_vault_statistics

tools.description: Scans for uncompleted tasks (lines containing '- [ ]') and returns them grouped by file

directory.description: Root directory to scan (default: current directory)

folder_scope.description: Optional subfolder path to restrict scan

tools.name: extract_active_todos

tools.description: Updates content under a specific heading in a Markdown file without touching the rest of the document

content.description: Markdown content to insert

heading.description: Heading name to target (without # symbols, e.g. 'Action Items')

mode.description: 'append' adds to bottom of section; 'overwrite' replaces everything under the heading

tools.name: safe_append_or_replace_section

tools.description: Validates a list of note names or file paths against real files on disk

directory.description: Root directory to search (default: current directory)

links.description: Note names, filenames, or paths to validate

tools.name: resolve_and_validate_links

tools.description: Inserts or overwrites a Markdown table under a specific heading. Creates file if it does not exist

heading.description: Section heading under which the table should be placed

path.description: Path to the Markdown file (will be created if absent)

rows.description: Array of rows, each an array of cell strings

tools.name: upsert_markdown_table

tools.description: Read raw file content as-is, without any conversion or processing

end_line.description: Last line to return (omit for full file)

path.description: Path to the file to read

tools.name: read_file

tools.description: Create a new file with content, or append/overwrite an existing file

content.description: Content to write or append

mode.description: Mode: 'create' (fails if exists), 'append' (add to end), 'overwrite' (replace entirely)

path.description: Path to the file to create or modify

tools.name: create_or_append_file

tools.description: Get help on available tools. Specify a tool name for detailed help, or omit for a summary of all tools

tool_name.description: Name of a specific tool (omit for full list)

tools.name: get_tool_help

tools.description: Move or rename a file or directory to a new path
tools.name: move_or_rename_file

tools.description: Permanently delete a file or directory (irreversible)

path.description: Path to file or directory to delete

recursive.description: Required true to delete non-empty directories (default: false)

tools.name: delete_file

tools.description: Create multiple new files in one call

content.description: File content

tools.name: batch_create_notes

tools.description: Update YAML frontmatter properties in a Markdown file without changing the body

properties.description: Key-value pairs to set or update (set value to null to remove)

tools.name: update_note_properties

tools.description: Fuzzy lookup to find a file by name, filename snippet, or frontmatter alias

directory.description: Root directory to search (default: current directory)

search_term.description: Conceptual name, filename snippet, or alias to search for

tools.name: find_note_by_alias_or_title

tools.description: Map wiki-link connections: outlinks (references this file makes) and backlinks (files that reference this file)

directory.description: Root directory to scan for backlinks (default: current directory)

tools.name: get_graph_relationships

tools.description: Split a Markdown/document file into heading-aware, token-bounded chunks for RAG/embeddings. Dual output: readable Markdown or structured JSON (output_format=json).

content.description: Inline Markdown/text to chunk instead of a file.

file_path.description: File to chunk (any supported format). Provide this or 'content'.

tools.name: chunk_markdown

tools.description: Convert any supported file to Markdown then chunk it into JSON records ({id, text, metadata}) ready for embedding. Primary ingestion entry point for RAG pipelines.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file.

tools.name: extract_chunks_for_rag

tools.description: Extract a nested heading outline (level/title/anchor/children) from a document. Dual output: Markdown list or JSON tree.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file.

tools.name: get_document_outline

tools.description: Search inside converted document content across a directory, ranked by term frequency. Returns ranked snippets with source and score. Dual output: Markdown or JSON.
tools.name: search_content

tools.description: Word/vocabulary statistics for a file: total words, distinct words, vocabulary richness, sentence/paragraph counts, and a per-word frequency table. Dual output: Markdown or JSON.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file.

top_n.description: Number of top words to report

tools.name: get_text_statistics

tools.description: Aggregate word statistics across a directory: per-document word/distinct counts plus corpus totals and global distinct-word count. Dual output: Markdown or JSON.
tools.name: get_corpus_statistics

tools.description: Extract #tags and frontmatter tags. With 'directory' builds a vault-wide tag index {tag, count, files}; with 'file_path'/'content' returns tags for one note. Dual output.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

directory.description: Vault directory to build a tag index from

file_path.description: Single file to extract tags from

tools.name: extract_tags

tools.description: Salient terms for a document via TF-IDF (pass 'directory' to use the vault as the IDF corpus). Answers 'what is this note about'. Dual output.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

directory.description: Optional corpus directory for IDF weighting

tools.name: extract_keywords

tools.description: Find notes similar to a given note via cosine similarity over TF vectors, boosted by shared tags/links. Powers 'see also' and RAG neighbor expansion. Dual output.

embeddings.description: Use vector embeddings instead of TF/SimHash heuristics (persistent per-directory index; falls back to hashed vectors when no model is available)

file_path.description: The note to find neighbors for

tools.name: find_related_notes

tools.description: Extractive TL;DR: rank sentences by keyword density and position and return the top ones. Deterministic, no LLM. Dual output.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

tools.name: summarize_document

tools.description: Mine Q:/A: lines and '?'-terminated headings into {question, answer, source} pairs — flashcards, eval sets, or RAG ground-truth. Dual output.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

tools.name: extract_qa_pairs

tools.description: Lightweight entity extraction: URLs, emails, dates, and capitalized name phrases, aggregated with counts. Dual output.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

tools.name: extract_entities

tools.description: Flagship 'second brain' export: one JSON artifact bundling summary, outline, tags, keywords, stats, and RAG chunks for a document — a single object an AI agent or vector DB can ingest to 'know' the note. Always JSON.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

tools.name: build_knowledge_index

tools.description: RAG retrieval: rank chunks across a directory (or file) against a query and assemble the top ones into one context block under a token budget, with citations. The retrieval step to feed an LLM. Dual output.

embeddings.description: Use vector embeddings instead of TF/SimHash heuristics (persistent per-directory index; falls back to hashed vectors when no model is available)

file_path.description: Single file to retrieve from instead of a directory

query.description: The question/topic to retrieve context for

tools.name: retrieve_context

tools.description: Estimate token count for a file/content and show whether it fits each model's context window (Opus 4.8, Sonnet 5, Haiku 4.5). Dual output.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

tools.name: count_tokens

tools.description: Detect near-duplicate documents across a directory using SimHash. Returns groups of similar files with a similarity score. Dual output.

embeddings.description: Use vector embeddings instead of TF/SimHash heuristics (persistent per-directory index; falls back to hashed vectors when no model is available)

min_similarity.description: Minimum cosine similarity to group (embeddings mode)

threshold.description: Max differing bits (0-64); lower is stricter (SimHash mode)

tools.name: find_duplicates

tools.description: Cluster documents in a directory by topic via cosine similarity over term vectors. Returns labeled groups with top terms. Dual output.

embeddings.description: Use vector embeddings instead of TF/SimHash heuristics (persistent per-directory index; falls back to hashed vectors when no model is available)

min_similarity.description: Minimum cosine similarity to join a cluster

tools.name: cluster_documents

tools.description: Compute Flesch Reading Ease and Flesch-Kincaid grade level with word/sentence/syllable counts. Dual output.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

tools.name: analyze_readability

tools.description: Detect the natural language of a document (English/Spanish/French/German/Portuguese/Italian) via function-word analysis. Dual output.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

tools.name: detect_natural_language

tools.description: Heuristic topic/content-type classification (technical, finance, legal, correspondence, academic, marketing) via keyword signals. Dual output. See ai_classify for the LLM version.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

tools.name: classify_document

tools.description: Abstractive summary via the Claude API (requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; returns a setup note if absent). Local alternative: summarize_document.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

style.description: Summary style, e.g. concise, detailed, bullet points

tools.name: ai_summarize

tools.description: RAG question-answering via the Claude API: retrieves relevant context across a directory/file, then answers grounded in it with citations (requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).

file_path.description: Single file to answer from instead of a directory

tools.name: ai_ask

tools.description: Suggest topical tags for a document via the Claude API (requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). Local alternative: extract_keywords.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

tools.name: ai_tag

tools.description: Translate a document to a target language via the Claude API, preserving Markdown (requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

tools.name: ai_translate

tools.description: Classify a document into caller-provided labels via the Claude API (requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). Local alternative: classify_document.

content.description: Inline content instead of a file

tools.name: ai_classify

tools.description: Open a URL in a real Chromium browser (executes JavaScript, keeps cookies/session). Headless by default; set visible=true to show the window so a human can solve CAPTCHAs, log in, or dismiss banners. The session stays open across tool calls — follow up with browser_capture_markdown to convert the rendered page, and browser_close when done.

visible.description: Show the browser window for human interaction (default: false = headless)

wait_seconds.description: Extra seconds to wait after page load for JS-rendered content to settle (default: 0)

tools.name: browser_open_url

tools.description: Capture the rendered HTML of the page currently open in the browser session (after any human interaction) and convert it to Markdown. Optionally pass a url to navigate first — with no prior session this opens a headless browser, so JS-heavy pages can be converted in a single call. Supports the same HTML processing options as convert_from_source.

convert_tables.description: Convert HTML tables to Markdown tables (default: false)

extract_metadata.description: Extract page metadata as YAML frontmatter (default: false)

generate_toc.description: Generate table of contents from headings (default: false)

image_format.description: Image output format: link, reference, or base64 (default: link)

preserve_comments.description: Preserve HTML comments with summary (default: false)

preserve_css_hints.description: Preserve CSS styling hints as HTML comments (default: false)

timeout_seconds.description: Navigation timeout in seconds when url is given (default: 30)

toc_max_level.description: Maximum heading level to include in TOC (1-6, default: 3)

url.description: Optional URL to navigate to before capturing (opens a headless session if none exists)

wait_seconds.description: Extra seconds to wait after navigation before capturing (default: 0)

tools.name: browser_capture_markdown

tools.description: Close the Chromium browser session opened by browser_open_url and free its resources.
tools.name: browser_close

tools.description: Index an Obsidian vault: note/attachment counts, tag frequencies, aliases, broken and ambiguous wikilinks, and optionally orphan notes. Understands [[link|alias]], [[note#heading]], [[note#^block]], ![[embeds]] and frontmatter aliases.

include_orphans.description: List notes with no inbound links (default: false)

vault_path.description: Path to the vault root directory. Optional when the server runs with --base-dir (defaults to the first base dir)

tools.name: obsidian_vault_index

tools.description: Get a note by path, filename stem, or frontmatter alias: parsed YAML frontmatter, aliases, tags (frontmatter + inline #tags), headings, outgoing wikilinks, backlink count, and content. Optionally transclude ![[embeds]] recursively.

inline_embeds.description: Replace ![[embeds]] with the embedded note content (default: false)

vault_path.description: Path to the vault root directory. Optional when the server runs with --base-dir (defaults to the first base dir)

tools.name: obsidian_get_note

tools.description: Resolve a wikilink string ([[target#heading|alias]], alias, path, or ^block form) using Obsidian's shortest-path rules. Reports Resolved/Ambiguous/Broken, whether the heading exists, and the block anchor line.

vault_path.description: Path to the vault root directory. Optional when the server runs with --base-dir (defaults to the first base dir)

tools.name: obsidian_resolve_link

tools.description: List all inbound wikilinks to a note — including alias, heading, block, and embed forms — with the linking note and the source line as context.

vault_path.description: Path to the vault root directory. Optional when the server runs with --base-dir (defaults to the first base dir)

tools.name: obsidian_get_backlinks

tools.description: Search an Obsidian vault by tag (with nested-tag prefix matching), alias, frontmatter field (key or key=value), or full text.

mode.description: One of: tag, alias, field, text

query.description: Search query; for field mode use 'key' or 'key=value'

vault_path.description: Path to the vault root directory. Optional when the server runs with --base-dir (defaults to the first base dir)

tools.name: obsidian_search

tools.description: List checkbox tasks across the vault or one note, with all states ([ ] open, [x] done, [/] in progress, [-] cancelled, [>] forwarded, ...), nesting depth, #tags, and Tasks-plugin dates (📅 due, ✅ done, ⏳ scheduled, 🛫 start, 🔁 recurrence).

note.description: Limit to one note (path, stem, or alias)

status.description: Filter: open, done, in_progress, cancelled, forwarded, or a raw state char

vault_path.description: Path to the vault root directory. Optional when the server runs with --base-dir (defaults to the first base dir)

tools.name: obsidian_list_tasks

tools.description: Read the vault's .obsidian configuration: attachment folder, new-link format, daily-notes folder/format/template, templates folder, and enabled core plugins.

vault_path.description: Path to the vault root directory. Optional when the server runs with --base-dir (defaults to the first base dir)

tools.name: obsidian_get_vault_config

tools.description: Create a note (or today's daily note per .obsidian/daily-notes.json) from a template, substituting {{title}}, {{date}}, {{time}}, and {{date:FORMAT}}. Refuses to overwrite existing notes.

daily.description: Create today's daily note using the vault's daily-notes settings (default: false)

template.description: Vault-relative template path (default: daily-notes template when daily=true)

title.description: Note title, optionally with folder ('projects/New Idea'). Required unless daily=true

vault_path.description: Path to the vault root directory. Optional when the server runs with --base-dir (defaults to the first base dir)

tools.name: obsidian_create_note_from_template

tools.description: Rename or move a note and rewrite every inbound wikilink (preserving |alias, #heading, and #^block fragments), like Obsidian's own rename. dry_run defaults to true — pass dry_run=false to apply.

new_name.description: New stem (same folder) or vault-relative path

note.description: Note to rename (path, stem, or alias)

vault_path.description: Path to the vault root directory. Optional when the server runs with --base-dir (defaults to the first base dir)

tools.name: obsidian_rename_note

tools.description: Convert an Obsidian .canvas file (JsonCanvas: text/file/link/group nodes and edges) to structured Markdown with groups, nodes in reading order, and connections.
tools.name: obsidian_convert_canvas

tools.description: Complete text metrics: word/character/space/token counts plus sorted frequency tables for words, characters, and tokens. Modular provider-aware tokenization — openai (tiktoken, exact), anthropic (cl100k proxy, estimate), meta/llama/qwen/deepseek (exact with a tokenizer.json via tokenizer_file, else estimate), grok (estimate), heuristic. Estimated counts are clearly flagged.

content.description: Inline text to analyze

file_path.description: File to analyze instead of inline content

model.description: Model hint for the provider (e.g. gpt-4o vs gpt-4 selects o200k vs cl100k)

provider.description: Tokenizer provider: openai, anthropic, meta, llama, qwen, deepseek, grok, heuristic (default: anthropic)

tokenizer_file.description: Path to a HuggingFace tokenizer.json for exact meta/qwen/deepseek counts

tools.name: analyze_text

tools.description: Extract Dataview fields — inline 'key:: value' (line, [bracketed], (parenthesized) forms) and frontmatter properties — across the vault or one note, optionally filtered by field name.

note.description: Limit to one note (path, stem, or alias)

vault_path.description: Path to the vault root directory. Optional when the server runs with --base-dir (defaults to the first base dir)

tools.name: obsidian_extract_dataview_fields
